title: Lance of Longinus Direct Combat
wikitext:
[]: Lance of Longinus: Direct Combat             Quote Post by BEsERk EVA01 » Sat Oct 13, 2007 3:58 am The Lance of Loginus is a tool used for The Human Instrumentality Project. Right? Well, of course it's going to be an important object, but couldn't they just use the Lance to destroy all of the Angels? I mean, call me stupid, but it would've saved them a lot of trouble with defeating the Angels and all. Evidently, your answer would be something like, "Well, DUH! It's so important they can't afford to use it". But I mean, an Eva Unit just needs to throw it at the target. I don't seem to think the angel could deflect it in any way if it can penetrate any unneutralized A.T Field. Any opinions on this? By the way, please do not be too angry or frustrated at me just because I'm a newbie here. I'm just curious. Besides, I actually thought I knew a lot about Shin Seiki but when I found this, I was dumbfounded. Anyway, please don't be so frustrated. :cry:

[]:             Quote Post by Indigo Arcangel » Sat Oct 13, 2007 4:17 am Don't worry, I feel your pain. It does seem that the reluctance to use the spear is simply because it is too important and precious. This is just demonstrated by the fact that when they use it, they lose it, and Seele gets suspicious - not to mention pissed off. I also heard somewhere that there was a reason for them impaling Lilith with it - something to do with halting her growth. I think it may be specified in the classified information: http://evageeks.org/FGP/Classified_Information_%28Translation%29 Anyone else have any thoughts on this?

[]:             Quote Post by BEsERk EVA01 » Sat Oct 13, 2007 4:22 am Yeah, you're right, but couldn't they just make another replica of the Lance? eg: The Mass Production Eva's Lances. They were constructed of the same matter and dimensions of the original version, and they could also puncture A.T Fields pretty quickly as demonstrated in End of Evangelion. Good point though.

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Sat Oct 13, 2007 4:24 am Put it this way- the only time the Spear is used, Gendo uses it in a manner meant to dispose of it. Of all the Angels, only Leliel, out of sheer bizareness, might have had any protection from the Spear. Bardiel and Zeruel would have had it the moment the twin prongs touched them. I agree with BobBQ's fanfic version of events...why the hell didn't they bring up the Spear to use against Zeruel? If Rei popped up with it instead of the N2 mine, it'd be...STAB! Dead Zer. Of course, the real problem seem to be the committee members, who even Gendo and Fuyutsuki admit complain about everything and care little about anything except their own security.

[]:             Quote Post by Indigo Arcangel » Sat Oct 13, 2007 4:37 am That's a good point. I suppose if they used the Lance too soon, it would look far too suspicious, and although Gendo does use it against Ariel, it is done in a situation where there were no other viable options. And even then, Seele were annoyed. If Gendo had used it earlier, in a situation where it was not necessary, Nerv would have been completely screwed over by Seele. Even in Tenant of Room 404, the comitee is frustrated when Smith uses the Lance.

[]:             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Sat Oct 13, 2007 5:34 am First of, it was never meant as a weapon. It's a security device that can suposedly think for itself, though that idea seems somewhat laughable. Why it killed Areal and only knocked Adam out is rather odd since its intended perpose is to place the Seeds in suspended annimation. I feel like yelling PLOT DEVICE in situations like this. Second, copying it isn't as easy as you make it sound. Remember it is alive and would need to be cloned like the Evas. This isn't something you can do overnight.

[]:             Quote Post by Indigo Arcangel » Sat Oct 13, 2007 8:09 am Indeed. They only just managed to get some lance clones ready in time for EoE. There are two possible reasons I can think of why the Lance killed Areal, but not Adam. On Areal, it was deliberately used as a weapon. On Adam, we don't know how it was used. Secondly, Adam was a seed of life, Areal was an Angel. Quite simply, the two types of entity could be affected differently by the Lance. As a second example, we see Lilith skewered, but she's not dead, because she's a seed of life.

[]:             Quote Post by Operation Yashima » Sat Oct 13, 2007 1:50 pm From Anime cafe's Law of Anime: 27. Law of Conservation of Firepower (from U. Williams) Any powerful weapon capable of destroying/defeating an opponent in a single shot will invariably be reserved and used only as a last resort.

[]:             Quote Post by BEsERk EVA01 » Sat Oct 13, 2007 7:38 pm Operation Yashima wrote: From Anime cafe's Law of Anime: 27. Law of Conservation of Firepower (from U. Williams) Any powerful weapon capable of destroying/defeating an opponent in a single shot will invariably be reserved and used only as a last resort.

[]:             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Sat Oct 13, 2007 8:14 pm There are too many unknowns in-play to throw the Spear around like it's an end-all answer. You can't use it agains't Sandy without losing it. Imagine Zeruel overpowering a damaged EVA-00 and stealing it, all would be lost at that point. Not to mention with it sitting in Lilith 3I is impossible because any Angel that fused with her would instantly be put in suspended animation. There's a lot more to think about besides kill the Angels .

[]: Re: Lance of Loginus: Direct Combat             Quote Post by chee » Sat Oct 13, 2007 11:08 pm It was too valuable to SEELE.

[]:             Quote Post by Indigo Arcangel » Sun Oct 14, 2007 2:11 am That's what I keep saying. As they say, you break it (lose it), you bought it.

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Mon Oct 15, 2007 9:09 am Certainly too invaluable to use as a weapon. Seele had major plans for it in 3I with Lilith and an ADO being contributing factors. With the elimination of the Lance Seele seemed force to introduce a fallible, human element into 3I - mainly in the form of Yui/Sho and Shinji. That's something they really didn't want, hence the protection of the Lance. Though I still have no clue as to what exactly Seele planned to do with the Lance, Lilith, and an ADO in 3I 'Plan A', and why that option became nullified with the elimination of the Lance. I think it has something to do with them needing Lilith in an 'unfinished' state - but I don't know why. Possibly because if they planned on re-introducing Lilith's soul into her original body they needed a means to control her, and the Lance was their only way to do that.

[]:             Quote Post by DatDude » Mon Oct 15, 2007 9:13 am they where working on copies of the lance thats why the mp evas had them. Also they might have been worried how lilith might react if it was removed

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Mon Oct 15, 2007 10:16 am Not to mention that for half the series the Lance was still in Antarctica.

[]:             Quote Post by V » Mon Oct 15, 2007 10:37 am 1-it was so vital to 3rd Impact that they were worried about using it in combat and something happening to it 2-they needed it stuck in Lilith to keep her growth in check; it wasn't a total problem when they removed it, as it would make her grow really big right away, but that did advance the time table

[]:             Quote Post by Defectron » Tue Oct 16, 2007 1:06 am In eoe when the lance clones intiailly look like those sword looking weapons, what do you think the deal is what that? Did seele somehow make the lances assume a secondery form? And would the real lance also be able to switch into that swordlike form as well?

[]:             Quote Post by chee » Tue Oct 16, 2007 1:09 am The Lance of Longius is a gigantic Twizzler sculpted into the shape of those plastic red forks you get at Nathan's.

[]:             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Tue Oct 16, 2007 1:10 am Seems like an obvious WTF moment set-up

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Tue Oct 16, 2007 1:15 am Defectron wrote: In eoe when the lance clones intiailly look like those sword looking weapons, what do you think the deal is what that?
